sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: 제너럴 다이내믹스는 2025년 2분기 매출 130억 4,100만 달러를 기록하며 견조한 방산 및 항공 사업 포트폴리오를 유지했습니다.

keyPoints.text: 미국 국방부 및 정보기관을 포함한 정부 고객 중심의 사업 구조를 통해 항공, 해양, 전투 시스템 및 기술 분야에서 안정적인 매출을 창출하고 있습니다.
항공 부문의 매출은 신규 항공기 인도 물량과 서비스 유형의 혼합에 따라 변동하며, 생산 로트별 평균 단위 원가 산정이 영업비용에 직접적인 영향을 미칩니다.
2025년 상반기 영업활동 현금흐름은 14억 5,000만 달러로, 전년 동기 536달러 대비 크게 개선되었습니다.

riskChanges.text: 항공 및 전투 시스템 부문의 운전자본 변동성이 현금흐름에 부정적인 영향을 미치고 있으나, 경영진은 이러한 시점 차이가 하반기에 해소될 것으로 예상합니다.
재무제표 작성 시 사용되는 추정치와 가정은 과거 경험과 현재 정보를 바탕으로 하며, 실제 결과가 이러한 추정치와 다를 수 있는 불확실성이 존재합니다.

businessChanges.text: 항공 부문은 신규 항공기 인도와 관련된 노동, 재료, 하도급 비용을 생산 로트별로 집계하여 인도 시점에 매출원가로 인식하는 구조를 유지하고 있습니다.
2025년 상반기 영업활동 현금흐름은 순이익이 주도했으나, 항공 및 전투 시스템 부문의 운전자본 증가로 인해 일시적인 현금 흐름 제약이 발생했습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 2025년 2분기 매출 130억 4,100만 달러, 영업이익 13억 500만 달러, 순이익 10억 1,400만 달러를 달성했습니다.
총자산은 568억 8,800만 달러이며, 현금및현금성자산은 15억 2,300만 달러를 보유하고 있습니다.

watchItems.text: 상반기 운전자본 부담으로 인한 현금흐름 변동이 경영진의 예상대로 하반기에 정상화되어 잉여현금흐름 개선으로 이어질지 확인이 필요합니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: Quarter ended 2025-06-29 revenue = 130억 4,100만 달러 (USD 13.041B; 13,041 million), XBRL range 2025-03-31..2025-06-29.
Quarter ended 2025-06-29 operating income = 13억 500만 달러 (USD 1.305B; 1,305 million), XBRL range 2025-03-31..2025-06-29.
Quarter ended 2025-06-29 net income = 10억 1,400만 달러 (USD 1.014B; 1,014 million), XBRL range 2025-03-31..2025-06-29.
Quarter ended 2025-06-29 total assets = 568억 8,800만 달러 (USD 56.888B; 56,888 million), XBRL range instant..2025-06-29.
Quarter ended 2025-06-29 cash and cash equivalents = 15억 2,300만 달러 (USD 1.523B; 1,523 million), XBRL range instant..2025-06-29.

MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS (Dollars in millions, except per-share amounts or unless otherwise noted) BUSINESS OVERVIEW General Dynamics is a global aerospace and defense company that offers a broad portfolio of products and services in business aviation; ship construction and repair; land combat vehicles, weapons systems and munitions; and technology products and services. Our company is organized into four operating segments: Aerospace, Marine Systems, Combat Systems and Technologies. We refer to the latter three collectively as our defense segments. Our primary customer is the U.S. government, including the Department of Defense (DoD), the intelligence community and other U.S. government agencies. We also have significant business with non-U.S. governments and a diverse base of corporate and individual buyers of business jet ai

Revenue associated with the segment’s services businesses is recognized as work progresses or upon delivery of services. Fluctuations in revenue from period to period result from the number and mix of new aircraft deliveries, and the level and type of aircraft services performed during the period. The majority of the Aerospace segment’s operating costs relates to new aircraft production on firm orders and consists of labor, material, subcontractor and overhead costs. The costs are accumulated in production lots, recorded in inventory and recognized as operating costs at aircraft delivery based on the estimated average unit cost in a production lot. While changes in the estimated average unit cost for a production lot impact the level of operating costs, the amount of operating costs reported in a given period is based largely on the number and type of aircraft delivered. Operating costs

Cash Flows in Part I, Item 1: Six Months Ended June 29, 2025 June 30, 2024 Net cash provided by operating activities $ 1,450 $ 536 Net cash used by investing activities (216) (307) Net cash used by financing activities (1,403) (778) OPERATING ACTIVITIES Cash provided by operating activities was $1.5 billion in the first six months of 2025 compared with $536 in the same period in 2024. The primary driver of cash flows in both periods was net earnings. Cash flows in both periods were affected negatively by growth in operating working capital, particularly driven by timing in our Aerospace and Combat Systems segments. These timing items were anticipated and are expected to reverse, resulting in higher cash flows from operating activities in the second half of the year. 38 INVESTING ACTIVITIES Cash used by   Normalized numeric aliases: $1.5 billion = 15억 달러; $1,450 = 1,450달러; $536 = 536달러; $1.5 = 1.5달러; $216 = 216달러; $307 = 307달러; 1,450 million = 14억 5,000만 달러; 1,403 million = 14억 300만 달러.

Results of Operations is based on the unaudited Consolidated Financial Statements, which have been prepared in accordance with GAAP. The preparation of financial statements in accordance with GAAP requires that we make estimates and assumptions that affect the reported amounts of assets and liabilities and the disclosure of contingent assets and liabilities at the date of the financial statements, as well as the reported amounts of revenue and expenses during the reporting period. We employ judgment in making our estimates, but they are based on historical experience, currently available information and various other assumptions that we believe to be reasonable under the circumstances. Actual results may differ from these estimates. We believe our judgment is applied consistently and produces financial information that fairly depicts our results of operations for all periods presented. A
